If ihe querist touches his forehead or sees corn with sharp
thorns (like paddy), it is to be inferred that he has eaten food con-
sisting of the Sastika rice (which ripens in sixty days) J and if he
touches the neck, his food consisted of barley.
The metre is

*5T TH WW w^lw
If he touches the abdomen, paps, stomach and knee, he must
have eaten black grain (with rice), milk (with rice), sesamura (with
rice) and rice gruel respectively ; if he licks his lips, he must have
enjoyed sweet dishes.
a** \
f^cj n^n
If he strikes his tongue against the corner of the mouth, it is
to be inferred that the querist had eaten something sour ; if he
makes his mouth wry, something pungent ; if he hiccups, something
astringent ; and if he spits, something salt.
